summary: Improvement cycle addressing the three findings from berendt's external review #2 (W-001, W-002, I-001). Two files in scope: (1) .github/workflows/ci.yaml gains `verify-invalid-cr-fixtures` in the `needs:` of `build-e2e-images` so the documented gating of the cluster-bound `e2e-operator` job is real rather than parallel; (2) tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/_generate.py adds (a) `encoding="utf-8"` on both `read_text`/`write_text` to make em-dash round-tripping locale-independent and (b) a bidirectional drift sweep using regex `^(0[2-9]|1[0-9])-.+\.yaml$` that flags or removes orphan YAML files no longer declared in `FIXTURES`. The remaining files in scope are .planwerk metadata (review record, progress phase, three review-pattern files capturing the lessons). Verified: `_generate.py --check` exits 0 against current tree; injecting a synthetic `13-fake-orphan.yaml` correctly triggers exit 1 with the orphan annotation; `python3 test_generate.py` runs all 4 tests green; ruff and `mypy --strict` clean on the changed file.

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases are covered (orphan detection verified by injection)
Locale-independent round-trip behavior locked in via encoding pin
Drift check returns 0 on clean tree, 1 on injected orphan

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (comments cite review IDs W-001/W-002/I-001 for traceability)
No dead code, no scope creep beyond the three review findings
Regex compiled at module level with descriptive private name and rationale comment
Deterministic iteration via sorted(here.iterdir()) for stable output

security_checklist.item: Filename regex is anchored and bounded; cannot match traversal sequences
path.unlink() only triggers on files matching the bounded regex inside the script's own directory

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is the minimal verbatim implementation of the reviewer's suggested fixes
Documented CI gating now matches actual dependency graph (build-e2e-images depends on verify-invalid-cr-fixtures, e2e-operator transitively depends on build-e2e-images)
Drift detection is now bidirectional (FIXTURES->disk and disk->FIXTURES)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic; orphan sweep reuses the same drift list and check_only flag

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected early (drift sweep runs in <1s in CI before any cluster job)
Orphan files now surface as drift instead of silently surviving

defensive_checklist.item: Encoding pinned on file I/O eliminates locale-dependent silent corruption
Orphan sweep restricted to script's own directory, file-only, regex-bounded — cannot delete unrelated files

suggested_improvements: Optional, non-blocking: the orphan-detection regex `^(0[2-9]|1[0-9])-.+\.yaml$` is bounded to filenames numbered 02-19. If FIXTURES ever grows past 19 entries the regex would silently stop matching new orphans. Currently FIXTURES has 10 entries (02-12) so this is many years off, but a follow-up could broaden to `^[0-9]{2}-.+\.yaml$` paired with an explicit allowlist for the pre-existing CC-0012 fixtures (00-, 01-) so the sweep stays consistent if the ID space expands.

Optional, non-blocking: the no-arg `_generate.py` invocation will now silently `unlink()` an on-disk file that a developer may have just hand-created before adding the matching FIXTURES entry. The `print(f"removed orphan {path.name}")` line provides feedback, and the workflow (edit FIXTURES first, then regenerate) makes this safe in practice — but a `--prune` flag or a confirmation prompt could be considered if anyone hits the footgun. Not worth changing now.
next_steps: Merge once the CI run on this branch shows verify-invalid-cr-fixtures green and build-e2e-images consuming it via the new needs: edge.

On parent issue #277, tick the nine in-scope checkboxes and link the three deferred prerequisite items (image.tag MinLength, topologySpreadConstraints maxSkew Minimum, clusterRef immutability on ValidateUpdate) to follow-up feat-tickets, as planned in task 4.3.
